_meta.note: Source of truth for the FFT:IVC item OVERHAUL. baseline = REAL vanilla/IVC values (Game8). proposed = the build-diversity rebalance values, emitted to the modloader ItemWeaponData XML by tools/generate.py. name = NEW overhaul name (-> item.en.nxd); vanillaName kept for reference. tier = availability rank (1=earliest .. 6=poach/endgame) -- a valid 'pick this' axis, enforced by analyze.py.
_meta.knifeFormula: (PA + Speed) / 2 * WP  -- speed-scaling; knives own the fast/agile/utility niche
_meta.renameTarget: item.en.nxd (name + description). NXD round-trip mechanism: WIP.
_meta.design: Three lanes run the curve in parallel: RAW (Cutpurse/Argent Dirk/Adamant Fang), AGILE (Quicksilver 25ev / Sanguine Gauche 50ev), UTILITY (Gloomfang Blind / Hushblade Silence / Mortal Coil Doom / Dreamsever Sleep). Non-monotonic WP (5,6,5,6,7,6,9,8,10,12) makes every proc/evade knife undercut WP to pay for its gimmick.
_meta.openProcs: id4 Hushblade Silence onHitAbilityId is unknown -- not one of the 9/11/12 procs. Confirm in-game before locking the description.
_meta.equipBonus: Armor/shield RIDERS reuse existing vanilla EquipBonus rows where possible (only 8 free ItemEquipBonusData slots: 74-79; 77 of 85 used). Each item's proposed.equipBonusId points at a vanilla id OR a free-slot id defined in _equipBonus below. The iconic element shields already exist: Ice=15, Flame=16, Kaiser-boost=18, Venetian-halve=19; stats: MA+1=10/12, MA+2=1/9, PA+1=21, PA+2=24, Speed+1=14/23, Move+1=42, Shell=4.

items.name: Cutpurse
items.identity: Honest Ch1 starter / cheapest dual-wield enabler -- earliest unlock is its niche
items.desc: A humble street knife, cheap and ready from your first day in the field.

items.name: Quicksilver
items.identity: Early AGILE knife -- real dodge identity (25 evade), not just 'a bit better than Cutpurse'

items.name: Gloomfang
items.identity: Anti-physical debuffer -- one hit blinds an enemy bruiser for the whole fight (only Blind source)
items.desc:
Etched with shadow. A solid hit leaves foes blinking in darkness.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Blind.

items.name: Hushblade
items.identity: Anti-caster -- shut an enemy mage's spellbook before they nuke (only Silence source). Proc id not yet pinned down in-game.
items.desc:
Forged to throttle spellcraft. A caster's worst nightmare.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Silence.

items.name: Argent Dirk
items.identity: Clean no-strings mid-game main-hand -- reliable damage + modest dodge, no gimmick

items.name: Sanguine Gauche
items.identity: Parry-tank lifeleech -- line-max evade (50) AND every hit drains HP (Night Sword, decimal 48 = 0x30; switches dmg to PA*WP). Two reasons it's never dominated.
items.desc:
A parrying blade that drinks the blood it spills.
Deflects with rare skill, and heals the wielder for damage dealt.

items.name: Adamant Fang
items.identity: Glass-cannon stat-stick -- best STRINGLESS buyable damage; pays with line-min evade (5) and zero rider

items.name: Mortal Coil
items.identity: Single-target deleter on a clock -- Doom countdown then guaranteed KO. WP8 sits UNDER Adamant Fang (pays for the proc).
items.desc:
Mark a foe and let the clock run; death keeps its appointment.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Doom.

items.name: Galewind
items.identity: Elemental main-hand -- top flat damage with a Wind exploit (x2 vs Wind-weak). Double-edged: Wind-absorbers heal.
items.desc:
Bound with a sliver of caged storm. Each strike rides the wind.
Deals Wind-elemental damage.

items.name: Zwill Straightblade
items.identity: Endgame capstone + burst-setup -- Sleep a target, team deletes it at +50%. Top WP but poach-gated and the Sleep self-removes, so a setup tool not a universal best.

items.name: Shadowfang
items.identity: Earliest ninja blade -- budget dodge main-hand: line-low WP, line-high 15% evade, only blade this early. Availability + evade is the pick.

items.name: Mistedge
items.identity: REACH outlier -- the only Range-2 melee in the line (throwing-knife fantasy). Hits from a tile back without melee retaliation; pays with line-min evade for its tier. Iconic name kept.

items.name: Frost Kodachi
items.identity: Ice arsenal -- first elemental blade; two Ice procs/turn under Dual Wield. Anti-Fire, +25% vs Ice-weak. Only Ice source; unlocks a tier before the Lightning blade so it can't be dominated by it.

items.name: Raijin Longblade
items.identity: Lightning arsenal + line-max 20% evade. Mid-game dodge-DPS: anti-mechanical / Lightning-weak coverage on the highest-evade chassis. Only Lightning melee source. Splits from Spellbinder (same tier/WP) by trading Silence for element + top evade.

items.name: Silentfang
items.identity: Anti-caster blade -- on-hit Silence shuts down enemy mages (vanilla Disable re-skinned to Silence id10, matching Regabonds). Pays with no element and base evade. Only on-hit Silence in the line. Iconic name kept.

baseline.onHit: Disable (Don't Act) -- VANILLA. ** Regabonds CHANGED to Silence (OptionsAbilityId=10) **

items.name: Sasuke's Blade
items.identity: Marquee ninja blade -- best-in-line 20% evade AND on-hit Sleep (vanilla Stop re-skinned to Sleep id12; both deny turns). Trades 1 WP under Iga for top evade + a soft-lock fired twice/turn. Iconic name kept.

items.name: Iga Blade
items.identity: No-strings DPS capstone -- line-max 14 WP, strong 15% evade, zero gimmick. Two 14-WP PA*WP hits/turn when you want raw damage over a tradeoff. The endgame default. Iconic name kept.

items.name: Koga Blade
items.identity: Attrition capstone -- only Dark-element blade + on-hit Poison DOT (Regabonds id51 re-skinned to Poison id22). Trades evade for relentless chip that ticks while the ninja dodges away. Fixes vanilla's strictly-dominated Koga. Iconic name kept.

baseline.onHit: None -- VANILLA. ** Regabonds SET OptionsAbilityId=51 (non-vanilla, non-confirmed) **

items.name: Vagabond
items.identity: Nimble budget starter -- lowest WP but line-best early evade (12). Dodge over hit; earliest unlock.
items.desc: A worn wanderer's blade. Cheap, light, ready from your first march.

items.name: Cleaver
items.identity: Heavy starter -- trades the Vagabond's guard for +2 raw WP. The early aggressor's pick.

items.name: Riposte
items.identity: Parry sword -- top evade of the line below Nagnarok (16). The defensive duelist; out-guards every higher-WP sword.
items.desc: A duelist's sword built to turn blows aside and answer them.

items.name: Claymore
items.identity: REACH SWORD test -- the only sword that strikes from 2 tiles, paid for with zero evade + no element/proc

items.name: Sanguine Sword
items.identity: Rare one-handed Sword (not sold, shield-friendly): casts Sanguine Sword (absorb HP) on hit -- the life-drain of a fell mercenary knight, echoing Gaffgarion. Lives on the NATIVE Blood Sword slot so the card correctly reads Sword (relocated off the Giant's Axe slot, which read Axe).
items.flavorOverride: Once the blade of a fell mercenary who drank the life of all he struck, until he too fell in battle.

items.name: Stormbrand
items.identity: Lightning blade -- attacks deal Lightning (x2 vs Lightning-weak; Lightning-absorbers heal it). Only Lightning sword.

items.name: Tanglethorn
items.identity: Pin-blade -- chance to inflict Immobilize (target can act but can't move). Pin runners; lock melee off your back line. Only Immobilize source. Proc id 14 unverified in-game.
items.desc:
An ancient blade that roots its victims where they stand.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Immobilize.

items.name: Flamberge
items.identity: Fire blade -- attacks deal Fire (anti-Undead, shreds Oiled/Ice foes; Fire-absorbers heal it). Re-themed off Sleep (knives own Sleep). Only Fire sword.
items.desc:
A wavering blade that burns like a torch.
Deals Fire-elemental damage.

items.name: Wrathblade
items.identity: Berserker sword: damage equals the wielder's missing HP. Zero at full health, devastating near death. WP and evade are cosmetic for damage. The widely-equippable desperation blade.

items.name: Swiftedge
items.identity: Speed-Knight blade (unique): damage = Speed x WP, not Physical Attack. Mediocre at base, devastating if you stack Speed.

items.name: Rimebrand
items.identity: Ice blade -- high WP, attacks deal Ice (anti-Fire-creature; Ice-absorbers heal it). Only Ice sword.
items.desc:
A blade of eternal frost; each cut bites with cold.
Deals Ice-elemental damage.

items.name: Arcanum
items.identity: Arcane dispel sword: chance to strip the target's buffs on hit (Protect/Shell/Haste/Reflect/etc.). The melee answer to buffed foes; Omnilex is the ranged version.

items.name: Nagrarok
items.identity: Evade-wall trickster -- line-max 50% physical evade AND on-hit Toad (target reduced to weak basic attack). Near-zero WP (3): dodge-and-disable, never damage. Proc id 16 unverified in-game.

items.name: Materia Blade
items.identity: Dark mage-knight capstone -- Dark element + absorbs MP every hit (battle-mage fuel). Solid WP11/Ev12. Still item-id 32 = Cloud's Limit weapon (function unchanged by rename). UiSE 1001.

items.name: Defender
items.identity: Parry-tank capstone -- game-max 60% physical evade, lowest WP of the five, EquipBonus rider Protect (halve physical taken). The un-killable front-line dodge wall; pick it to NOT die, not to deal damage. Most available of the five (tier 4). Renamed from generic 'Defender' to signal the pure shield-blade role.

baseline.formula: Knight Sword (PA*Brave/100*WP); XML Formula field=1, Brave-scaling is hardcoded by weapon type

items.name: Save the Queen
items.identity: Disabler-bruiser -- strong 45% evade plus Sleep-on-hit to neuter a target while you out-tempo the duel, backed by an EquipBonus Regen rider for self-sustain. The 'control the fight' pick between pure tank (Aegisbrand) and the Holy capstone. Sleep proc owned by no other sword. Renamed from 'Save the Queen' for the warden identity.

items.name: Excalibur
items.identity: Holy-knight tempo capstone -- Holy element (anti-Undead, with its signature Holy absorb) plus EquipBonus rider Haste for extra turns. The build-defining matchup + tempo + survivability blade; the only elemental Knight Sword that also carries a permanent rider. Iconic name kept. Seated at vanilla WP21 (verbatim Game8/Regabonds) mid-ladder.

baseline.formula: Knight Sword (PA*Brave/100*WP); XML Formula field=1, Elements=Holy

items.name: Ragnarok
items.identity: Anti-caster prestige blade -- unique Dark element plus Silence-on-hit shuts down enemy mages, with high WP and middling evade. Fixes vanilla's 'squeezed slot' (riderless WP24, dominated by Chaos): now owns the Dark + Silence niche so it is NOT dominated by Chaos Blade. Iconic name kept. The pick versus caster-heavy comps.

items.name: Chaos Blade
items.identity: Raw-power king -- highest WP in the category, lowest evade (paid defensively), Doom-on-hit (death-timer) plus EquipBonus rider Regen for sustain. The element-less brute-force capstone; pick it to delete a target. Vanilla WP50 game-breaker cut to WP28 so it no longer strictly dominates Ragnarok; the unreliable Stone/Petrify proc swapped for Doom (id 11), thematically on-point. Iconic name kept.

baseline.onHit: Stone/Petrify proc on hit (chance) + EquipBonus rider PERMANENT REGEN

items.name: Ashura
items.identity: Earliest katana + the line's evade crown (25%) -- the defensive samurai's first blade; out-dodges every higher-WP katana, earning its slot on availability + guard, not power. No rider to misfire while you learn the class.

items.name: Kotetsu
items.identity: Cheap anti-bruiser -- one swing Blinds an enemy melee threat for the fight (only early Blind katana). Pays -7 evade vs Asura for +1 WP and the rider.

items.name: Bizen Osafune
items.identity: MP-siphon blade (Formula 47 = Absorb-MP) -- every hit drains the target's MP and refuels the wielder's own Iaido casts. The mage-samurai / Draw-Out fuel battery. Same WP as Murasame; identity is the MP economy. Set UiStatusEffectId=1001 (absorb convention).

items.name: Murasame
items.identity: The rain blade made literal -- chance to perform Draw Out: Ashura on the struck enemy (formula 2 cast). Where Bizen FUELS Iaido (MP drain), Murasame PERFORMS one on hit. No other weapon casts a Draw-Out; pure samurai fantasy. Formula 2 re-scales the strike to PA*WP (clean T2 sidegrade, +3 evade over Bizen).
items.flavorOverride: The rain blade, whose every cut recalls an Iaido it was never taught.

items.name: Ame-no-Murakumo
items.identity: The gathering-storm blade -- only Wind katana (x2 vs Wind-weak) AND Silences on hit, choking an enemy mage's spellbook. The anti-caster capstone of the mid line (Heaven's Cloud lore). Trades evade for the storm package.

items.name: Kiyomori
items.identity: Attrition parry-blade -- the evade apex below the capstones (20%) plus Poison-on-hit that chips high-HP foes every clock tick (its Protect/Shell Draw Out reframed as relentless pressure). Survives any duel; outlasts raw blades vs bulk.

items.name: Muramasa
items.identity: Berserker katana (the Muramasa curse): damage equals the wielder's missing HP. Zero at full health, up to a near-MaxHP hit near death. WP and evade are cosmetic for damage. A desperation finisher.

items.name: Kiku-ichimonji
items.identity: The reach blade -- the ONLY range-2 katana, echoing Kikuichimonji's long-range Draw Out. Strike from the second rank and stay safer; no element or status, reach IS the identity. Nothing at this WP can match its range.

items.name: Masamune
items.identity: The legendary all-rounder -- high WP AND top-tier evade AND innate Holy AND Sleep-on-hit soft CC (its Haste/Regen Draw Out rendered honestly as battlefield tempo control). Poach-from-Elmdore rarity (T5). Trades 4 WP for the whole package, so it never dominates Chirijiraden. Holy-absorbers heal.

items.name: Chirijiraden
items.identity: Endgame damage capstone -- max WP (20) and Immobilize-on-hit that pins the target so it can't flee the carnage (its MA*30 ultimate Draw Out reframed as 'nowhere to run'). Pays for top damage with middling guard, no element, and pure endgame-only availability (T6). A sidegrade to Masamune, not a strict upgrade.

items.name: Terrastaff
items.identity: Earth pole: the only Earth-element pole, for the Geomancer who lost the Axe. (was the Hatchet)

baseline.formula: 1 (Weapon formula; engine applies Axe random-damage Rdm(1..PA)*WP by weapon type)

items.name: Ravager
items.identity: Berserk brute -- high-WP striker with on-hit Berserk: drives the struck foe into a mindless rage (shuts off their abilities/spells, but they hit +50% harder). Only Berserk source mod-wide; an anti-caster/anti-healer gambit. Repurposed Giant's Axe slot; relabeled KnightSword via UiItemCategoryId + a KnightSword icon so the card no longer reads Axe (pending in-game verify).
items.flavorOverride: A brutal weapon that infects all it cuts with its own blind, mindless fury.

items.name: Executioner
items.identity: Executioner knight sword: pins the condemned where they stand, chance to Immobilize on hit. (was Headsman's Axe)

baseline.onHit: Slow (chance) — OptionsAbilityId 18 in Regabonds; Game8 IVC confirms 'chance of inflicting Slow'

items.name: Apprentice Rod
items.identity: Only tier-1 rod -- cheapest, earliest, max rod evade. Earliest unlock is its exclusive niche.

items.name: Spark Rod
items.identity: Only Lightning rod -- anti-armor/mech matchup; sidegrade trio w/ Ember/Frost, element is the sole difference.

baseline.onHit: chance to cast Thunder spell on hit

items.name: Ember Rod
items.identity: Only Fire rod -- anti-ice-creature / hit Fire weakness. Equal-WP sidegrade, picked for element coverage.

baseline.onHit: chance to cast Fire spell on hit

items.name: Frost Rod
items.identity: Only Ice rod -- anti-fire-creature / anti-dragon. Completes the early elemental trio.

baseline.onHit: chance to cast Blizzard spell on hit

items.name: Venom Rod
items.identity: Cheapest Poison source -- colorless DoT to grind HP-fat/fleeing targets. Trades 5 rod evade for the proc.

items.name: Umbral Rod
items.identity: Only Dark caster weapon AND the MA+2 EquipBonus rider -- black-mage power stick; anti-Holy-creature niche.

items.name: Dragon Rod
items.identity: Silence on hit, plus the only weapon that grants Initial Reraise (auto-revive once per battle). A survivable battle-mage rod, echoing the dragoon Dragon Spirit (community-requested buff).

items.name: Rod of Faith
items.identity: Only Holy rod -- smite undead + self-Faith glass-cannon rider. Top rod, element is the draw.

baseline.onHit: chance to inflict Faith on target on hit; EquipBonus permanent Faith on wielder

items.name: Birchwood Staff
items.identity: Only tier-1 staff -- bare MA-scaling starter. Not a worse Apprentice Rod: MA*WP scaling is its incomparable good.

items.name: Lullaby Staff
items.identity: Only Sleep source -- white-mage pacifier, removes a target non-lethally. Earliest status staff.

baseline.onHit: chance to REMOVE Doom on hit (cure rider)

items.name: Mending Staff
items.identity: Only heal-on-attack weapon (Formula 7) -- attack allies to heal, attack Undead to nuke. WP held low so the heal identity stays load-bearing.

items.name: Viper Staff
items.identity: Only Immobilize source -- the serpent's coil pins runners in place. MA-scaling status pick; pays WP5 with ev12.

baseline.onHit: None per Game8 IVC (classic FFT = Poison rider; unconfirmed in IVC)

items.name: Conduit Staff
items.identity: Mid MA+1 EquipBonus staff -- clean caster-power pick before endgame, full 15 evade. MA+1 != MA+2.

items.name: Smiter's Staff
items.identity: Pure-WP no-rider staff -- the 'biggest MA*WP swing now' pick. Pays 3 evade vs the MA staves and carries no proc.

items.name: Zeus Mace
items.identity: Hybrid gish staff -- only Lightning staff + MA+1 rider, full evade. Treasure-gated; element is a good the capstone lacks.

items.name: Staff of the Magi
items.identity: Endgame capstone -- highest WP, top MA+2 rider, AND only Doom source. T6 gate earns the WP gap; dominates nothing (no element vs Zeus, later tier than Mage's).

items.name: Warbrand
items.identity: Pure-power brute: the highest weapon power of any sword, bought with zero evade. (was Ironlash)

items.name: Bloodlash
items.identity: Venom dagger: chance to Poison on hit, a bleeding wound that festers. (was a flail)

baseline.onHit: Chance to cast Fira (OptionsAbilityId 17). Game8 IVC: 'chance of casting Fira'
baseline.formula: 2 (Elemental weapon formula — REQUIRED to carry Fire element; NOT formula 1)

items.name: Snarefang
items.identity: Snare blade: chance to Immobilize on hit, pinning the target where it stands. (was Snarelash)

baseline.onHit: None (VANILLA) — Regabonds also has OptionsAbilityId 0. Brief's Immobilize(14) is a DESIGN TARGET, not vanilla.

items.name: Sasori
items.identity: Venom katana: a scorpion's lethal sting, chance to Doom on hit (death in three turns). (was Scorpion Tail)

baseline.onHit: None (VANILLA, per Game8 IVC: no status). Regabonds ADDS OptionsAbilityId 22 (Poison) — that is a Regabonds change, NOT vanilla. Brief's Poison(22) target matches Regabonds, not pure vanilla.

items.name: Outrider Pistol
items.identity: Tier-1 starter and the LONGEST reach in the game (R9). Plain WP*WP (49 flat) ignoring PA/Speed/evade, no element to whiff or be absorbed, no drawback, earliest/cheapest gun. The out-range-everything pick that never retires; reach + availability ARE the niche.

baseline.formula: 3 (decimal) - gun physical, damage = WP*WP, ignores PA/Speed

items.name: Ironclad Repeater
items.identity: Mid-shop reliable workhorse: +2 WP over the Outrider (81 flat) for -1 range. Plain WP*WP, the only element-proof AND Faith-independent gun above the starter; never whiffs on Faith-immune targets, no absorber heals off it. The zero-gimmick hit-hard-at-range default.

baseline.formula: 3 (decimal) - gun physical, damage = WP*WP, ignores PA/Speed

items.name: Stoneshooter
items.identity: Drawback-paid power spike: top-of-line WP16 (256 base) AND tied-longest R9, fires EARTH (only Earth gun; ignores Float, shreds grounded). PRICE: the wielder begins EVERY battle Petrified, the iconic Stoneshooter curse, demanding a Maintenance/esuna plan. Highest ceiling but the deadliest to field, so it never free-lunches the clean capstone.

baseline.onHit: None on TARGET. DRAWBACK: wielder starts battle in Stone (Petrify) status.
baseline.formula: 3 (decimal) - gun physical, damage = WP*WP, ignores PA/Speed

items.name: Glacial Gun
items.identity: First elemental gun: Ice, WP12 (144 base), R8. Formula-4 innate Blizzard-line cast, Faith-scaled (a high roll beats the Repeater, a low/immune roll loses). The only Ice gun: anti-Fire-creature, +25% vs Ice-weak, but Ice-absorbers heal it. Element + Faith variance is the trade for the WP jump.

baseline.onHit: Inherent chance to cast Blizzard/Blizzara/Blizzaga (Ice spell) on hit - this is the elemental-gun magic-cast, NOT a status rider.
baseline.formula: 4 (decimal) - elemental gun, damage = WP*WP, Ice-element via Elements field

items.name: Blaze Gun
items.identity: Fire twin of the Hailshot at IDENTICAL WP12/R8: the different element is the entire differentiator. The only Fire gun: anti-Undead, anti-Ice-creature, shreds Oiled targets; Fire-absorbers heal it. A true sidegrade, never an upgrade, picked purely for the Fire matchup.

baseline.onHit: Inherent chance to cast Fire/Fira/Firaga (Fire spell) on hit - elemental-gun magic-cast, NOT a status rider.
baseline.formula: 4 (decimal) - elemental gun, damage = WP*WP, Fire-element via Elements field

items.name: Blaster
items.identity: Endgame capstone: Lightning, WP15 (225 base), no drawback, only Lightning gun (+25% in rain; Lightning-absorbers heal). PAYS for its clean power with the SHORTEST gun range (R7) so every other gun out-distances it: the close-the-gap-and-nuke finisher, not a kiter. The poach/endgame trophy.

baseline.onHit: Inherent chance to cast Thunder/Thundara/Thundaga (Lightning spell) on hit - elemental-gun magic-cast, NOT a status rider.
baseline.formula: 4 (decimal) - elemental gun, damage = WP*WP, Lightning-element via Elements field

items.name: Scoutbolt
items.identity: T1 scout's sidearm -- earliest/cheapest unlock + line-high 10% evade. Shortest range (3), lowest WP.
items.desc:
A light scout's crossbow, quick to the shoulder and cheap to replace.
Effective range: 3 tiles.

items.name: Eclipsebolt
items.identity: Assassin's bolt -- marks an elite for death. Proc 21 is Blind, not Death, so it uses Doom (id 11) instead -- a countdown to a certain KO. Only Doom crossbow.

items.name: Arbalest
items.identity: Raw stringless workhorse and the ONLY Range-5 crossbow. Best rider-free WP of its era + unique reach; the clean PA*WP sniper.
items.desc:
A heavy steel crossbow with unmatched reach. Pure, reliable punch.
Effective range: 6 tiles.

items.name: Venombolt
items.identity: Attrition tool -- only Poison crossbow. WP undercuts Arbalest and gives up Range-5, so the DoT IS the value; best vs high-HP/fleeing targets. Inflicts Poison (proc 22).
items.desc:
A crossbow that coats its bolts in venom.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Poison.
Effective range: 5 tiles.

items.name: Pitchbolt
items.identity: Combo-setup bolt -- proc 101 applies Oil, which doubles the Fire damage the target takes; pairs with any Fire attacker. Line-high 8% evade. Only Oil source.
items.desc:
A crossbow that douses its target in clinging pitch.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Oil (the target takes double Fire damage).
Effective range: 7 tiles.

items.name: Siegebolt
items.identity: Endgame raw-power capstone -- top crossbow WP (11), no rider. At PA9+ it out-damages bows on a strength build; surrenders all utility for damage.
items.desc:
A massive siege crossbow. All the stopping power a strong arm can drive.
Effective range: 8 tiles.

items.name: Skirmisher
items.identity: T1 light scout -- HIGHEST evade in the line (20) + earliest/cheapest. Shortest range (5), lowest WP. The nimble dodge-opener.
items.desc:
A short, nimble bow for hit-and-run scouts. Quick draw, quicker feet.
Effective range: 5 tiles.

items.name: Windrunner
items.identity: T2 dodge duelist -- 2nd-best evade (15) + colorless = safe vs any element-absorber. True sidegrade to Frostarc (same WP5: evade+safety vs element).
items.desc:
A featherlight silver bow that dances out of reach. No element, no risk.
Effective range: 6 tiles.

items.name: Frostarc
items.identity: T2 first elemental -- attacks deal Ice (anti-Fire-creature; Ice-absorbers heal it). Trades Windrunner's evade for element at equal WP5.
items.desc:
A bow strung with frostsilk; each arrow bites with cold.
Deals Ice-elemental damage.
Effective range: 4 tiles.

items.name: Stormarc
items.identity: T3 elemental -- attacks deal Lightning (anti-armor/robotic; Lightning-absorbers heal it). +1 WP over Frostarc, different element coverage.
items.desc:
A bow humming with static; arrows crack like thunder.
Deals Lightning-elemental damage.
Effective range: 7 tiles.

items.name: Skypiercer
items.identity: T4 the REACH bow -- innate Wind + longest non-capstone range (7); arcs over cover, picks off fliers/cliff-campers. Glass: 0 evade, all reach.
items.desc:
A long-limbed bow that arcs arrows over any wall.
Deals Wind-elemental damage; longest reach of the buyable bows.
Effective range: 8 tiles.

items.name: Tidecaller
items.identity: T3 caster-lock / control bow -- Silence on hit shuts enemy mages at range. WP7 + 10 evade. Renamed from filler Mythril Bow. Inflicts Silence (proc 10).
items.desc:
A bow whose arrows steal the voice.
When attacking, has a chance of inflicting Silence.
Effective range: 5 tiles.

items.name: Huntress
items.identity: T5 marksman / mid-evade owner -- WP9 + 10 evade, earlier than the capstones. Out-dodges Tempest, out-shoots the mids. Fixes vanilla 'Yoichi dominates Artemis'.
items.desc:
A balanced marksman's bow. Power and poise in equal measure.
Effective range: 6 tiles.

items.name: Yoichi Bow
items.identity: T6 AGILE capstone -- top sustained WP (12) that STILL keeps 5 evade: heavy damage on light feet. The speed-build DPS capstone vs Seraph's glass cannon.

items.name: Perseus Bow
items.identity: T6 raw+reach capstone -- max WP (15) + max range (8) + innate Holy (anti-Undead). 0 evade = pure glass cannon.

items.name: Siren's Lyre
items.identity: T2 elemental opener harp -- the family's only Wind source: Formula-1 Wind attacks punish Wind-weak/Float foes at R3. Hybrid (PA+MA)/2 bard; element is the rider, WP modest. Earliest harp.

items.name: Duskstring Harp
items.identity: T3 anti-physical harp -- the only ranged Blind in the harp lane: crater an enemy bruiser's hit-rate from 3 tiles back without waking them. Owns Blind.

baseline.onHit: Absorb HP (Formula 6 -- heals attacker for damage dealt)

items.name: Faerie Harp
items.identity: T5 capstone harp -- iconic faerie lullaby: ranged Sleep lulls a target out of the fight for a team burst (self-removes on hit, so a setup tool not a delete). Top harp WP, T5-gated. Owns Sleep line-wide.

items.name: Glarebound Tome
items.identity: T1 orator opener -- earliest/cheapest book and the only ranged Blind in the Book lane: neuter an enemy physical attacker from afar. Lowest WP in the category; the accuracy-denial rider is the point.

baseline.onHit: Casts Trepidation (AoE Bravery-down; vanilla Foxbird) -- Formula 2

items.name: Knell Codex
items.identity: T4 execution codex -- the only Doom source line-wide: tag an elite, retreat, let the 3-turn countdown guarantee the KO. Owns Doom.

baseline.onHit: Casts Fervor (vanilla Death Sentence / Praise-family) -- Formula 2

items.name: Binding Codex
items.identity: T4 pin codex -- ranged (R3) Immobilize locks a runner/charger in place for your casters and archers. Book-lane Immobilize (R3/Ev15 pin-from-range), distinct from the cloth pinner.

items.name: Omnilex
items.identity: T6 anti-caster capstone -- ranged Silence shuts an enemy mage's spellbook before they nuke, plus innate Holy attacks (anti-Undead; Holy-absorbers heal). Top book WP, T6-gated, owns Silence + Holy.

items.name: Skewer
items.identity: T1 starter spear -- earliest/cheapest reach, line-only 15% spear evade. Promoted to R2 (the family identity; vanilla R1 was a trap). The honest first poke + Dragoon Jump fodder.

items.name: Footman's Spear
items.identity: T2 raw-stat baseline -- cheapest pure-stat reach, clean PA*WP. The 'just poke hard from a tile back' default every other spear is measured against.

items.name: Frostpoint
items.identity: T3 only Ice spear -- innate Ice melee, WP tied to Footman's (zero raw paid for the matchup tool). Shreds Fire-creatures/Ice-weak; Ice-absorbers heal (cost).

items.name: Stormpike
items.identity: T3 only Lightning spear -- innate Bolt melee, +1 WP over Frostpoint (sidegrade, different element). Anti-armor/Lightning-weak; +25% in rain.

items.name: Tombspire
items.identity: T4 control spear -- only Earth spear + on-hit Immobilize (14). Pins a fleeing target a tile away, then the Dragoon Jumps. The zoning tool; carries two unique riders.

items.name: Holy Lance
items.identity: ICONIC. T5 only Holy spear -- innate Holy melee, anti-Undead crusader. The element does the work, no proc/Formula-2 needed. Holy-absorbers heal (cost). Sekhret-poach gated.

items.name: Dragon Whisker
items.identity: ICONIC capstone. T6 riderless raw apex -- strictly-highest spear WP (16), the ultimate Jump platform. Pure PA*WP ceiling; pays with the lowest 10% evade and zero utility. Red-Dragon poach.

items.name: Wyrmpike
items.identity: T6 split-apex (Nelveska treasure) -- only Wind spear + on-hit Doom (11), two-handed (trades the shield for the package). Tag a boss from two tiles back. Wins element/Doom where Dragon Whisker wins WP; neither dominates.

items.name: Greenwood Pole
items.identity: ICONIC. T1 starter pole -- earliest/cheapest MA-reach, the 20% evade family floor. The honest training staff for early Geomancer/Monk.

items.name: Ironreed Pole
items.identity: ICONIC. T2 raw-stat pole baseline -- best no-rider WP of its era at 20% evade. The 'monk who just wants to hit from a tile back' default.

items.name: Slumber Rod
items.identity: T3 only Sleep pole -- on-hit Sleep (12), WP tied to Battle Bamboo (zero raw paid for the disable). Nap a threat to open a fight from range.

items.name: Hushfan
items.identity: T3 only Silence pole + line-best 25% evade -- on-hit Silence (10). The dodge-tank anti-caster stick; shut a mage down from a tile back. Sidegrade to Slumber Rod.

items.name: Sage's Pole
items.identity: ICONIC. T5 riderless raw-WP pole leader -- clean MA*WP, no gimmick. The high-MA finisher that just wants to hit hard; tier-gated above the rider-poles.

items.name: Ivory Pole
items.identity: T4 only Blind pole -- on-hit Blind (9) craters a target's physical hit-rate. Sits under Gokuu's WP: the rider is the whole identity. Neuter an enemy bruiser from range.

items.name: Eight-Fluted Pole
items.identity: ICONIC. T6 attrition pole -- highest physical-pole WP (14) + only Poison rider (22). Chips high-HP targets over a long fight. Out-WPs Gokuu's but pays via tier + DoT-vs-burst tradeoff.

items.name: Whale Whisker
items.identity: ICONIC capstone. THE caster pole -- highest pole WP (15) + only Water element; MA*WP comes free from the Pole weapon type (Formula 1, no risk). Best-in-slot for a high-MA front-liner. Tiamat-poach.

items.name: Sandman Satchel
items.identity: Female/Onion-Knight bag, the soft-disable opener. Low WP but Sleep-on-hit (confirmed id12) removes a target from the fight outright. Owns Sleep across the bag toolbox. Tier 2 (bags genuinely unlock Ch2+, never Ch1) keeps it strictly distinct from the Ch1 Hatchet even in a forced single-bucket check. Gender/job-locked, so it never competes with an axe in practice.

baseline.formula: 1 (Weapon formula; Bag random — IVC Bag uses Rdm(1..PA)*WP same as axes/flails per DamageFormulas wiki)

items.name: Fallingstar Bag
items.identity: The bag ceiling king. Highest bag WP (vanilla's WP20 poach-outlier re-curved to a gentle 18, preserving its 'strongest bag' identity), no rider -- the female 'just hit hardest' pick when you don't want a status. Poach/late-game availability. Pays for its ceiling with zero utility, the bag mirror of War Axe. Owns the raw-WP niche in the bag set; can't dominate Sandman/Hornet/Hexweave because it lacks every one of their status riders.

items.name: Hornet Pouch
items.identity: Female bag attrition tool. Poison applicator (confirmed id22) for units who can't wield the endgame Scorpion Tail flail -- the niche is gender/job access, not raw power. Lower WP than Scorpion Tail but a separate equip class entirely; owns nothing the other bags own (Sleep/Silence/raw-ceiling), so no bag dominates it.

items.name: Hexweave Bag
items.identity: Female anti-caster bag. Silence-on-hit (confirmed id10) shuts down enemy mages -- the only Silence applicator in the whole bludgeon category. Mid-high bag WP, gender/job-locked. Owns Silence so no other bag dominates it; doesn't dominate Hornet Pouch (lacks its Poison) or Fallingstar (lower WP).

items.name: Tarsilk Veil
items.identity: T3 combo-enabler rag -- the only Oil source: Oil doubles incoming Fire, priming a target for your Black Mages while the dancer hides behind 50% evade. R2 short reach is the cloth trade.

items.name: Bindsilk Veil
items.identity: T4 lockdown rag (renamed from Cashmere) -- Immobilize behind a 50% evade wall: pin a target so your team kites it while she dodges everything. Cloth-lane Immobilize (R2/Ev50 evasion-fortress), non-comparable to the R3/Ev15 book pinner.

items.name: Gravesilk Veil
items.identity: T6 capstone caster rag -- chance to cast Gravity/Demi on hit (formula 2), chunking a flat share of the target's current HP regardless of defenses. The only Gravity source mod-wide; %HP scaling IGNORES the dancer's low PA, the rare offense vector that works despite her stat sheet. Keeps the 50% evade fortress.
items.flavorOverride: Silk that falls like a tombstone; even giants sag beneath its weight.

items.name: Tideward
items.identity: Mag-leaning T1 starter -- only earliest shield with real magEv, plus a cheap anti-Water resist.
items.desc:
A shield warded against the tides.
Halves Water-elemental damage.

items.name: Galewall
items.identity: T1 physical apex -- highest early physEv + anti-Wind resist for early archers/cyclones.
items.desc:
A broad shield that breaks the wind.
Halves Wind-elemental damage.

items.name: Stormwall
items.identity: First buyable element tool -- halves Lightning (guns, thunder mages). Balanced split.
items.desc:
A shield that grounds the storm.
Halves Lightning-elemental damage.

items.name: Swiftguard
items.identity: Agility shield -- phys-leaning evade + Speed+1 tempo prize for skirmishers.
items.desc:
A light shield that never slows your step.
Grants Speed +1.

items.name: Wardstone
items.identity: Anti-mage generalist -- top buyable magEv + innate Shell (stacks with mag-evade).
items.desc:
A rune-warded shield humming with protective magick.
Grants innate Shell (magick damage reduced).

items.name: Sanctguard
items.identity: Anti-Holy + Holy-amp -- halves incoming Holy AND boosts the wearer's own Holy output.
items.desc:
A consecrated shield that turns holy wrath aside, and amplifies your own.
Halves incoming Holy and strengthens your Holy attacks.

items.name: Rimeward
items.identity: Vanilla standout -- absorbs Ice, halves Fire, weak to Lightning. Anti-Ice/Fire tank.
items.desc:
A shield of everlasting frost.
Absorbs Ice, halves Fire, but weak to Lightning.

items.name: Emberward
items.identity: Vanilla standout -- absorbs Fire, halves Ice, weak to Water. Ice/Flame element partner.
items.desc:
A shield forged in dragonfire.
Absorbs Fire, halves Ice, but weak to Water.

items.name: Spellbane
items.identity: THE anti-mage shield -- 50 magEv + MA+1 + nullify Earth. Near-zero phys is the cost.
items.desc:
A mage's bane, woven to swallow spellcraft.
Huge magick evasion, grants Magick Attack +1, nullifies Earth.

items.name: Trailblazer
items.identity: Mobility defender -- solid balanced evade + the only Move+1 shield. Tank that flanks.
items.desc:
A shield light enough to range ahead of the line.
Grants Move +1.

items.name: Vanguard
items.identity: Offensive bruiser -- high physEv + the only PA+1 shield. Defense that adds damage.
items.desc:
A bruiser's shield. Defense that hits back.
Grants Physical Attack +1.

items.name: Nightward
items.identity: Anti-Dark -- absorbs Dark (undead bosses), weak to Holy. Best-balanced absorb shield.
items.desc:
A shield that drinks the dark.
Absorbs Dark, but weak to Holy.

items.name: Genji Shield
items.identity: Phys-evade apex -- poach/steal-gated, with a Speed+1 prize for the rare-drop effort.

items.name: Kaiser Shield
items.identity: The only OFFENSIVE shield -- strengthens the wearer's Fire/Lightning/Ice. A damage shield.

items.name: Venetian Shield
items.identity: Best generalist -- top on both evade axes + tri-element halve, no weakness, no rider.

items.name: Aegis Prime
items.identity: Endgame anti-mage capstone -- 56 magEv, MA+1, halves Holy/Dark.
items.desc:
The ultimate aegis, proof against blade and spell alike.
Grants Protect and Shell, with unmatched magick evasion.

items.name: Padded Coif
items.identity: Availability: the only riderless helm, alone at the bottom of the curve -- the head item armor jobs get turn 1 before any rider gear exists.

items.name: Mendsteel Helm
items.identity: Niche: innate Regen makes an early front-liner a self-healing wall before any healer is online -- attrition durability the raw-HP rungs never offer.

items.name: Aegis Helm
items.identity: Niche: innate Protect (halve physical) on a solid early block -- the front-line tank's first real wall; pick over Bronze vs hard hitters, not chip.

items.name: Storm Barbut
items.identity: Niche: nullifies Lightning entirely -- the slot's one element answer; hard-counters gun-heavy maps and Bolt/Summon mages where raw HP wouldn't save you.

items.name: Clarion Helm
items.identity: Niche: Silence immunity keeps a magic-knight's or battle-skill user's spells online -- the spell-using tank's helm, no element commitment.

items.name: Sunsteel Helm
items.identity: Niche: innate Float dodges Earth magic and ground traps and ignores swamp/terrain damage -- a positioning tool, not raw defense; the only mobility helm.

items.name: Sealed Visor
items.identity: Niche: immune Sleep and Blind -- the anti-control HP wall; keeps a front-liner awake and accurate when casters try to park or blind it off its hits.

items.name: Timeward Helm
items.identity: Niche: Stop immunity -- the Time-Mage counter; the heavy tank that can never be frozen out of the fight for a lethal turn-skip.

items.name: Wardsteel Helm
items.identity: Niche: innate Shell (halve magic) on a heavy block -- the anti-mage wall, magic mirror of Iron's Protect; the rung you grab for caster-boss maps.

items.name: Warded Circlet
items.identity: Niche: Charm immunity -- no friendly fire; 100 HP for a heavy unit that absolutely must stay loyal against mind-control bosses.

items.name: Crystalward Helm
items.identity: Niche: the buyable defensive apex -- double-immunity to Stop AND Petrify (both instant-loss) on a huge HP pool; owns the unique Stone atom no other helm has.

items.name: Genji Helm
items.identity: ICONIC: the legend, finally earning it -- rare/steal-gated, granting BOTH innate Protect and Shell (halve ALL incoming damage) on near-apex HP.

items.name: Grand Helm
items.identity: ICONIC: the heavy-armor HP capstone (150, slot ceiling) plus Doom/Sleep immunity -- no death-clock, no nap; the brick that can't be cheesed off the field.

items.name: Roughspun Cap
items.identity: Availability: cheapest head item, universal (any job turn 1, no Equip Heavy Armor) -- the mage/thief head-slot floor, alone at the bottom of the hat curve.

items.name: Wardplume Hat
items.identity: Niche: trades MP for magic-evade (magEv5) -- the cheap 'duck the first spell' hat; riderless but distinct on the magEv axis, a sidegrade not a downgrade to Red Hood.

items.name: Adept's Hood
items.identity: Availability: the early caster cap -- a small MP pool, HP-leaning twin of Plumed; the squishy mage's first survivable head, distinct on the MP axis.

items.name: Martial Band
items.identity: Niche: PA+1 with the highest HP of the early hats -- the cheap melee hat for a Monk/Archer wanting hat mobility plus a little punch; Headband is the budget-to-cap fork.

items.name: Arcanist Cap
items.identity: ICONIC: MA+1 plus a real MP pool, lowest HP of its tier -- the classic caster fork, glassy but more and stronger spells; the MA/MP answer to Martial Band's PA.

items.name: Zephyr Beret
items.identity: ICONIC: Speed+1 -- the universal tempo hat; more turns on literally any build, no stat-school commitment.

items.name: Twisted Headband
items.identity: ICONIC: PA+2, the physical-apex hat -- the Monk/Brawler damage cap; Martial Band (PA+1, T3) keeps the budget niche below it.

items.name: Hierophant Miter
items.identity: ICONIC: MA+2 plus a deep MP well -- the mage damage-and-fuel cap; Wizard's Hat (MA+1, T3) keeps the budget niche. The magic counterpart to Headband.

items.name: Assassin's Cowl
items.identity: Niche: physical-evade (physEv8) plus Move+1 -- the rogue/skirmisher hat; reposition and dodge, a mobility-plus-evade lane no other hat offers.

items.name: Mana Coronet
items.identity: ICONIC: the MP throne of the hats (40, slot max) plus Silence immunity -- the artillery-mage hat; bottomless spells that can't be cut off. MP is the identity.

items.name: Lumen Crown
items.identity: ICONIC: the hybrid endgame mage hat -- MA+1, Speed+1, MP AND magic-evade (magEv10); the only hat blending magic offense, tempo and mag-defense on one head.

items.name: Nightrunner Cap
items.identity: ICONIC: the rogue capstone -- Speed+2, top physical-evade (physEv15), immune Disable + Immobilize; the untouchable, unstoppable skirmisher hat.

items.name: Hairband
items.identity: Female partial immunity, half A: +20 HP + immune the 9 'degrade/lock' statuses. One of only 3 HP accessories; the affordable female defense before Ribbon. Disjoint set from Barrette.

proposed.rider: immune Doom, Disable, Immobilize, Slow, Poison, Toad, Silence, Blind, Undead

items.name: Barrette
items.identity: Female partial immunity, half B: +20 HP + immune the 9 'death/mind-control' statuses (incl KO and Petrify). The disjoint other half; together with Hairband equals Ribbon coverage. Neither half dominates the other.

proposed.rider: immune KO, Stone, Confuse, Vampire, Berserk, Stop, Charm, Sleep, Traitor

items.name: Ribbon
items.identity: Total immunity to every status. The crown jewel - deliberately the LOWEST HP (10) so the +20 half-sets stay viable when you only need one cluster + bulk. Loses the HP axis, so it dominates neither half-set.

items.name: Roughhide Mail
items.identity: Honest Ch1 starter wall -- cheapest heavy body; earliest unlock IS its niche. A riderless anchor protected purely by being the only heavy armor you can have in Ch1.

items.name: Studded Mail
items.identity: Attrition body -- a free Regen tick outlasts a slow poke-war; the early sustain pick for a front-liner who trades blows. (vanillaName Linen Cuirass)

items.name: Reaver Mail
items.identity: Aggro starter plate -- the only early heavy body that pushes Knight/Dragoon damage; offense over a few HP.

items.name: Hushward Mail
items.identity: Anti-hush body -- a melee fighter who can never be Silenced; counters enemy mage packs that lock your skillset.

items.name: Stalwart Plate
items.identity: Clean mid-game HP wall -- pure tonnage, no rider tax; the cheap 'just give me bulk' anchor of T3.

items.name: Knight's Plate
items.identity: Front-line bulwark -- innate Protect soft-halves all physical damage; the dedicated physical-tank body. (vanillaName Plate Mail)

items.name: Sungold Mail
items.identity: Pyre-ward plate -- heals from Fire (geomancers / enemy Fire mages) but takes extra Water (EB16 weakness); a true defensive sidegrade to Knight's Plate. (vanillaName Golden Armor)

items.name: Adamant Plate
items.identity: Riderless HP throne of the buyable line -- max bulk-per-gil one tier before the T5 wall; pick when you want raw HP and nothing else.

items.name: Warden Plate
items.identity: Anti-lockdown body -- immune Stop keeps your tank acting vs Time mages; tempo insurance and the cheaper anti-Stop pick under Crystal Mail. (vanillaName Platinum Armor)

items.name: Charger's Plate
items.identity: Tempo plate -- a rare +Speed on heavy armor; a tank that takes more turns. The only body that buys initiative.

items.name: Crystalward Mail
items.identity: Petrify-proof crystal -- immune to Stop AND Stone; hard counter to Break/Petrify plus Time-mage lockdown; top buyable HP.

items.name: Genji Armor
items.identity: Samurai/Knight offensive capstone -- top-tier HP PLUS PA+2; the rare plate for a damage build, not just a wall. Iconic name preserved.

items.name: Mirrorscale Mail
items.identity: The Reflect body -- bounces enemy single-target magic back at the caster; the only Reflect armor. Iconic name preserved.

items.name: Maximillian
items.identity: HP throne -- the single highest body HP in the game (160, well under the 255 cap for headroom), now with innate Shell to blunt the magic raw HP can't dodge; the endgame wall. Iconic name preserved.

items.name: Wayfarer's Garb
items.identity: The one riderless floor. Cheapest, available first, the bare body everything above earns its keep against. Pick when nothing else is affordable yet.

items.name: Emberweave Vest
items.identity: Early Fire-resist body (reuses vanilla EB16) -- absorb Fire, halve Ice, slightly weak to Water. The budget anti-Bomb/anti-Fire-mage pick before any heavy resist exists.

items.name: Trailwarden Jerkin
items.identity: Nimble light body -- +1 Move for skirmishers and archers. The early mobility pick when positioning beats raw HP.

items.name: Wardweave Vest
items.identity: Traveler's tri-element ward (reuses vanilla EB19) -- halves all three primary elements. The broad-resist budget body for mixed-element fights.

items.name: Tempo Vest
items.identity: Tempo body -- +1 Speed to act sooner each round. The light-armor speed pick before Ninja Gear exists. Pick when you want more turns out of a unit.

items.name: Wardsilk Vest
items.identity: Caster-safety body -- never get Silenced out of your spells. Pick when your mage must keep casting through enemy Silence.

items.name: Mage's Wrap
items.identity: ICONIC MP body (Wizard Clothing) -- +18 MP and +1 MA. The mage's light armor: trades HP for spell uptime + a touch of power. MP+MA is the whole identity, so its lower HP is never a strict loss.

items.name: Bulwark Brigandine
items.identity: Durable front-line cloth -- innate Protect halves incoming physical. Heavy-armor-like survivability with universal access and no Equip ability. The light bruiser's wall.

items.name: Adept's Gi
items.identity: ICONIC Monk body (vanilla EB30) -- +1 PA and immune to instant-death (Death/KO). Pick for a melee unit that must not get one-shot by Death spells.

items.name: Berserker's Garb
items.identity: ICONIC +PA bruiser body (vanilla EB31) -- the clean physical-attack stat stick. Pick when maximizing Monk/Ninja/melee damage.

items.name: Terraweave Garb
items.identity: ICONIC Earth body (vanilla EB32) -- heal from Earth attacks and boost your own Earth/Geomancy, plus a little MP. Pick for Earth builds or against enemy Earth nukes.

items.name: Ninja Gear
items.identity: ICONIC assassin body (vanilla EB33) -- pure agility: +2 Speed and start each battle Invisible, traded for deliberately low HP. End-tier rare for a fast striker who opens from stealth.

items.name: Nightweave Garb
items.identity: Anti-Stop durability body (vanilla EB34) -- the highest-HP light body that can never be time-locked out of the fight. The near-pure HP wall.

items.name: Rubber Suit
items.identity: ICONIC Rubber body (vanilla EB35) -- fully null Lightning, high HP and some MP. End-tier rare. Hard counter to Thunder/Bolt fights or a hybrid-caster anchor.

items.name: Penitent's Robe
items.identity: T1 starter caster body -- cheapest, earliest robe. Lowest HP/MP in the line but the ONLY robe with innate Regen (EB5): heals every turn. The budget attrition mage that out-sustains a plain riderless stat-stick in early poke wars.

items.name: Acolyte Robe
items.identity: T2 early-offense sidegrade to the Penitent's -- trades Regen sustain for +1 Magic Attack (EB10). The first damage robe; heal-over-time vs hit-harder-now is the early fork. Earliest MA in the slot.

items.name: Magus Robe
items.identity: T3 pure spell-power body -- +2 Magic Attack (EB36), zero defensive frills. Biggest flat MA in the slot; the black/white/time-mage damage default through mid-game. The OFFENSE half of the T3 fork with Chameleon.

items.name: Vesper Robe
items.identity: ICONIC. The SURVIVAL half of the T3 fork -- absorbs Holy (heal off enemy/ally Holy) and immune to instant-KO (EB37). Pick vs Holy-slinging foes (Knights Templar, undead) or Death-spam fights. More HP/MP than Magus but gives up its MA+2: offense vs survival, neither dominates.

items.name: Wardlight Robe
items.identity: ICONIC. The element-DEFENSE robe -- halves all three primary attack elements (EB19). The durable caster who walks into enemy Fire/Bolt/Ice crossfire. More HP than its Black twin (defense = more body) at a touch less MP.

items.name: Channeler's Robe
items.identity: ICONIC. The element-OFFENSE twin of White Robe (same tier, same 3 elements, opposite intent) -- boosts the WEARER's own Fire/Lightning/Ice spells (EB18). Glass cannon: more MP than White (more spells to throw) but less HP and no resist. Pick to nuke harder, not survive.

items.name: Empyrean Robe
items.identity: T5 MP throne -- highest MP short of the capstone. Its vanilla rider slot was empty; repurposed to innate Float (EB46): dodge Earth/Quake/floor-trap damage and ignore terrain. The battlefield-control caster body -- a mobility/immunity trait even Lordly lacks, so it stands on its own axis, not just availability.

items.name: Lordly Robe
items.identity: ICONIC capstone (rare/poach, T6). Top HP & MP of the line PLUS PA+2/MA+1 and innate Protect+Shell (EB41) -- the king's robe, best all-round body in the game. Endgame-gated so its raw superiority never dominates the T1-5 robes you wore earlier; tier IS the protecting axis, and every lower robe owns a rider trait Lordly doesn't have.

proposed.rider: PA+2, MA+1, innate Protect, innate Shell

items.name: Wayfarer Boots
items.identity: Earliest Move source. The cheap scout/flanker sandal; wins on tier-1 availability.

items.name: Cragstep Boots
items.identity: The Jump+1 vertical-reach pick. Orthogonal to Wayfarer (vertical vs horizontal); same tier.

items.name: Roamer's Boots
items.identity: Mobility king: Move+1 AND Jump+1. Pure positioning for thieves/Ninjas; pays its mid tier vs the cheaper singles.

items.name: Storm-Tread Boots
items.identity: Anti-Bolt / anti-pin tech boot. Not mobility at all; a hard counter to Lightning casters and Immobilize lockdown.

items.name: Featherfoot Boots
items.identity: Permanent Float: ignores Earth/quake and all floor traps, walks water/lava as level. The terrain-immunity boot (no Move number).

items.name: Fleetfoot Shoes
items.identity: The only tempo boot: Speed+1 = more turns, the scarcest stat in the slot. Endgame-gated; competes with the whole slot for raw tempo.

items.name: Sprinter's Shoes
items.identity: The mage's boot: MA+1 AND Move+1. The only boot touching a damage stat; reach for casters without spending the glove slot.

items.name: Ironhand Gauntlets
items.identity: Entry PA stick, bumped to PA+2 so it isn't trash next to Bracers. Cheap early physical boost for any martial job.

items.name: Genji Gloves
items.identity: The only dual-stat glove (PA+2/MA+1, magic-knight leaning physical). Less PA than Bracers (3>2), less MA than Magepower (2>1), subset of neither. Wins on breadth + the iconic steal.

items.name: Runecast Gloves
items.identity: The caster's stat stick: the only MA+2 source in the slot. A pure mage prefers this over Genji (more MA).

items.name: Titan Grips
items.identity: The PA apex: PA+3, the only +3 stat in the slot. The pure-physical capstone; gives zero MA.

items.name: Mirror Ring
items.identity: Permanent Reflect: bounces single-target magic back at casters; also bank-shots buffs off allies. The anti-mage staple.

items.name: Warding Ring
items.identity: Early anti-clock ring: never Doomed (countdown KO) or Slept. Cheap Ch2 status insurance.

items.name: Sealward Ring
items.identity: The caster's seal-breaker: never Silenced (can't lose spells) or Berserked (can't lose control). Keeps a mage online.

items.name: Cursed Ring
items.identity: Dark Pact ring (unique): a loaded PA+2/MA+1/Speed+2/Move+1/Jump+1 stat bundle that absorbs Dark, paid for with a Holy weakness. High risk/reward build-around (community-requested buff).

proposed.rider: PA+2, MA+1, Speed+2, Move+1, Jump+1, absorb Dark, weak Holy

items.flavorOverride: A ring forged in a forbidden pact. It floods the wielder with power and drinks the dark that strikes them, but holy light scorches the cursed twice as deep.

items.name: Seraph Ring
items.identity: Survivability crown (unisex): auto-Reraise + immune KO/Blind. The male answer to Chantage; the 'won't stay dead' ring.

items.name: Adamant Bangle
items.identity: The only stat armlet: +1 PA AND +1 MA plus immune Slow. Hybrid stat + tempo with no Undead drawback; a safe middle vs the Cursed Ring gamble.

items.name: Stoneward Armlet
items.identity: Anti-petrify: immune Stop (turn-locked) and Stone (instant loss). The 'you literally can't act' insurance vs Time mages.

items.name: Sanctus Beads
items.identity: The 108-gems armlet: immune the four 'corruption' statuses AND strong to all 8 elements (every element heals you) - which amplifies elemental damage taken too. Bizarre element-soak grail, dominates no pure-defense armlet.

proposed.rider: immune Undead, Vampire, Toad, Poison, boost all elements

items.name: Clearmind Armlet
items.identity: Anti-mind-control: never Charmed or Confused (no turning on your team) plus halve Dark. The only armlet mixing immunity with element resist.

items.name: Unbound Armlet
items.identity: Anti-lockdown: never Disabled (can't act) or Immobilized (can't move). The budget 'stay in the fight' armlet.

items.name: Drifter's Cape
items.identity: The starter balanced cloak (10/10). Earliest evade in the whole slot; equal phys/mag, asks nothing. Only T1 evade.

items.name: Duelist's Cape
items.identity: The anti-physical cloak (18 phys / 6 mag). Trades mag-dodge for cheap strong phys evade; the front-line dodge vs melee maps.

items.name: Runecloak
items.identity: The caster cloak: mag-leaning evade (12/18) PLUS MA+1. The only cloak with a stat rider; dodge spells AND hit harder. Lower phys is the tradeoff.

items.name: Sylvan Shroud
items.identity: The phys-leaning mid cloak (25/15). The all-rounder dodge tank's pick; forks against Vampire on which axis you need.

items.name: Nocturne Cape
items.identity: The anti-magic cloak (20 phys / 25 mag) PLUS innate Regen. Mirror of Elven; self-sustaining dodge vs caster-heavy maps. Does not dominate Elven (forks on axis + Regen vs raw phys).

items.name: Zephyr Cloak
items.identity: The phys-evade apex (35/20). Dedicated melee-dodge capstone; trails the mag-cloaks on magic evade, no rider - raw phys-avoidance is the sell.

items.name: Invisibility Cloak
items.identity: Starts each battle Invisible (untargetable + crit on the break) plus 30 phys evade. magEv 0 is the deliberate hole. The assassin/opener cloak; does not dominate Featherweave (less phys, zero mag).

items.name: Chantage
items.identity: The immortality perfume (female): permanent Regen + auto-Reraise. The 'never dies' throne; stronger than Angel Ring because Regen heals the Reraise back up.

items.name: Envoûtement
items.identity: The zoner perfume (female): Float + Reflect. Ignores terrain AND bounces magic in one slot. Female-gated separate pool, so it never strictly dominates the unisex Reflect Ring / Featherfoot Boots.

items.name: Septième Sens
items.identity: The aggressive caster perfume (female): MA+1 + Haste + opens Invisible. The burst-mage / opener throne. Female-gated, so never dominates a unisex item.

items.name: Sortilège
items.identity: The bulwark perfume (female): Protect AND Shell (-50% physical AND magical). The defensive wall; turns a fragile caster into a brick.

items.name: Scholar's Ring
items.identity: Doubles JP earned. Zero combat value - a pure progression item; swap on while grinding, off for fights. Owns its lane outright.
